questions: What is the correct sequence of implementation steps for adding a PX logo to the sign-in page?
What files need to be created or modified?
What verification checks are needed to confirm the implementation is correct?
Are there any cross-file dependencies or ordering constraints?

answers.answer: The implementation has three sequential steps: (1) Create the SVG logo asset at public/assets/images/px-logo.svg, (2) Modify the sign-in page component to import and render the logo above the Clerk <SignIn> component, (3) Run all quality gates to confirm nothing is broken. Step 2 depends on Step 1 (the import target must exist). Step 3 depends on Step 2.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md: 'Files changed: 1 new file (SVG), 1 modified file (sign-in page)'
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md: 'Two things are missing: (1) Logo asset, (2) Logo rendering'

answers.answer: Exactly two files: (1) NEW: public/assets/images/px-logo.svg — a simple text-based SVG with 'PX' lettering, (2) MODIFIED: src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx — add Image import from next/image, static import for px-logo.svg, and wrap <SignIn> in a flex-col container with the logo above it. No other files are touched.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md Summary Table: 'Files changed: 1 new file (SVG), 1 modified file (sign-in page)'
product/product.md: 'Shared layout caution: changes should be made at the page level, not the layout level'

answers.answer: Nine required checks: (1) typecheck passes, (2) lint passes, (3) dependency check passes, (4) i18n check passes, (5) unit tests pass, (6) browser verification showing PX logo on sign-in page, (7) browser verification confirming sign-up page is unaffected, (8) SVG asset exists and is valid, (9) only expected files are changed.
evidence: AGENTS.md: quality gate commands are lint, check:types, check:deps, check:i18n, test
product/product.md: success criteria include sign-up page unaffected and all quality gates pass
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md: success criteria include logo displayed, next/image pattern, sign-up unaffected

answers.answer: The only ordering constraint is that the SVG file (Step 1) must be created before modifying the sign-in page (Step 2), because Step 2 adds a static import that references the SVG file path. Step 3 (quality gates) must run after both file changes are complete. There are no cross-repo dependencies — all changes are within next-js-boilerplate.
evidence: The static import 'import pxLogo from @/public/assets/images/px-logo.svg' requires the file to exist at build/typecheck time

statement: The implementation plan covers three sequential steps: (1) create a PX logo SVG asset, (2) modify the sign-in page to display the logo above the Clerk sign-in form using next/image, and (3) verify all quality gates pass. Nine required verification checks cover type safety, linting, dependency analysis, i18n compliance, unit tests, browser-based visual verification of both sign-in and sign-up pages, SVG asset validity, and change scope. The plan modifies exactly two files in a single repository with no new dependencies.
